title: Durga temple, Aihole
tagline: Durga temple, Aihole — spiritual town of Karnataka

overview.short: The Durga temple is an early 8th-century Hindu temple located in Aihole, Karnataka, India.
overview.description: The Durga temple is an early 8th-century Hindu temple located in Aihole, Karnataka, India. Originally dedicated to Surya, it has the most embellished and largest relief panels in Aihole depicting artwork of Shaivism, Vaishnavism, Shaktism and Vedic deities. Apart from its fine carvings, it is notable for its apsidal plan – a rare example among early Chalukyan Hindu temple architecture. Though dedicated to Surya, the temple is now named Durga because a durg or fortified lookout was constructed on top of it after the 13th century during the wars between Hindu kingdoms and Islamic Sultanates.

topPlaces.name: Chalukya Shiva Temple
topPlaces.description: The Chalukya Shiva Temple, also known as Lad Khan Temple, dedicated to Shiva, is one of the oldest Hindu temples and is located in the group of monuments at Aihole in the state of Karnataka, India. Formerly dated to the 7th or 8th century, it is now dated to about the 5th century.

topPlaces.name: Aihole
topPlaces.description: Aihole (ಐಹೊಳೆ), also referred to as Aivalli, Ahivolal or Aryapura, is a historic site of ancient and medieval-era Buddhist, Hindu and Jain monuments in Karnataka, India that dates from the sixth century through the twelfth century CE. Most of the surviving monuments at the site date from the 7th to 10th centuries. Located around an eponymous small village surrounded by farmlands and sandstone hills, Aihole is a major archaeological site, featuring over 120 stone and cave temples spread along the Malaprabha river valley, in Bagalakote district.

topPlaces.name: Sulibhavi
topPlaces.description: Sulibhavi is a village in the southern state of Karnataka, India. It is located in the Hungund taluk of Bagalkot district in Karnataka.

topPlaces.name: Aminagad
topPlaces.description: Aminagad is a village in the southern state of Karnataka, India. It is located in the Hungund taluk of Bagalkot district in Karnataka.

topPlaces.name: Guledgudda
topPlaces.description: Guledgudda is a town in the Indian state of Karnataka. It was earlier a part of the Badami taluk, but is now the headquarters of the Guledgudda taluk in Bagalkot district.

topPlaces.name: Gudura
topPlaces.description: Gudura is a village in Bagalkot district in Karnataka.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Durga temple, Aihole?
faq.a: The best time to visit Durga temple, Aihole is Oct – Mar. Summers are 26–40°C and winters are 12–28°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Durga temple, Aihole?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Hubli Airport (112 km) and the nearest railway station is Hubballi Junction (110 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Durga temple, Aihole?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Durga temple, Aihole at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Durga temple, Aihole?
faq.a: Stays in Durga temple, Aihole start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Durga temple, Aihole?
faq.a: The top attractions are Chalukya Shiva Temple, Aihole, Sulibhavi, Aminagad, Guledgudda.
